obligation_text: For high-risk AI systems, where the provider has applied harmonised standards or common specifications, the provider shall opt for a conformity assessment procedure based on: the internal control referred to in Annex VI; or the assessment of the quality management system and the assessment of the technical documentation with the involvement of a notified body referred to in Annex VII.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI conformity assessment procedures, including internal control assessment methodology (Annex VI), notified body assessment processes (Annex VII), harmonised standard application, and common specification compliance demonstration.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI conformity assessment procedures, including internal control assessment methodology (Annex VI), notified body assessment processes (Annex VII), harmonised standard application, and common specification compliance demonstration.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI conformity assessments under the EU AI Act, including risk classification determination, technical documentation evaluation, and compliance assessment against applicable high-risk AI system requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 43(1) (obligation) equal WIDAI RC domain. RC-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.659, cosine: 0.770). Secondary: RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.729), AG-K-008 (AG, cosine: 0.706).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.6/10 (raw 0.658). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessment, compliance, conformity]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [annex, application, body, common, control, demonstration] vs WIDAI terms [act, against, applicable, assessments, classification, conducting] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.878 (P=0.869, R=0.888). Balanced P/R — the 27-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.770 > STS 0.658 (gap +0.112). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, assessment, compliance, conformity] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessment, compliance, conformity]. EU AI Act-only: [annex, application, body, common, control, demonstration]. WIDAI-only: [act, against, applicable, assessments, classification, conducting].
